Understanding Modern Vector Search

Posted on December 15, 2023 by Alex Chen

Vector search has become a cornerstone of modern information retrieval systems. In this post, we explore the fundamental concepts behind approximate nearest neighbor search and how it powers semantic search applications.

What is Vector Search?

At its core, vector search involves representing data as high-dimensional vectors (embeddings) and finding the most similar vectors to a query. Unlike keyword search, which matches exact terms, vector search captures semantic meaning.

HNSW Algorithm

Hierarchical Navigable Small World (HNSW) graphs provide an efficient data structure for approximate nearest neighbor search. The algorithm builds a multi-layer graph where each layer contains a subset of the points from the layer below.

Key parameters include the construction parameter M (maximum number of connections per node) and ef_construction (the size of the dynamic candidate list during construction).

Practical Considerations

When implementing vector search in production, consider memory usage, index build time, and query latency. For most applications, HNSW provides an excellent balance of speed and recall.
